work_unit: prod-deploy/report-event batch-index NaN guard (PD-L002 derived fix)
situation: audit-2026-07-03-013 finding PD-L002: report-event.js L227 parseInt(batch-index) returns NaN when --batch-index is missing or non-numeric. NaN propagates to payload.batch_index; findBatchEvent uses strict === match (event-client.js L99), so NaN never matches any existing event → duplicate deploy_batch events created per invocation. Silent data corruption in deploy tracking.
action: Added eager validation of parseInt result with Number.isNaN check; on invalid input, writes audit-log (deploy-batch-malformed-index, result=failure) per DO B sensitive-path rule and exits with clear error message. Prevents NaN from entering payload and breaking dedup logic.
outcome_evidence: Commit 5d1899f on auto/opt-prod-deploy-1782970212 pushed to origin. Route-fix reused existing prod-deploy worktree (same area as prior PD-* fixes). Single file changed, no public interface touched.
next_suggestion: Next derived fix: PD-H003 (priority 7, direction-shift) — poll-build.js + poll-pre-check.js zero audit-log for pipeline/precheck polling. Adds new ACTION_MAP keys (public interface .claude/public-interfaces.txt) so opt-worktree will auto-open new worktree. After that: PD-M001 (http-base dual credential silent win), PD-M003 (observation gate silent skip), PD-M004 (sunfire error homogenization), PD-L003 (extractIdFromLine regex too loose). When all 6 pending cleared, audit-cycle-state flips to cycle-complete → triggers next full audit.
